contextprompt
How it works Features Pricing FAQ Get Started Free

Code review meetings that produce tasks, not notes

Every comment made in a code review session deserves a ticket. contextprompt joins your call on Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams, transcribes every suggestion, and maps each one to the exact file and function it targets. Walk out of the review with a ready-to-execute task list, not a pile of scattered notes.

Get started free

No API keys needed. Free plan includes 1 hour/month.

Why code review feedback gets lost

Code review discussions are rich with technical detail. That detail rarely survives the journey to a ticket tracker.

//

Verbal feedback has no paper trail

A reviewer says “we should extract that validation logic into its own helper before we ship”. Nobody writes it down. Two weeks later, the PR merges without it. Verbal code review feedback that lives only in someone’s memory has a low survival rate.

?!

Action items lack context when filed later

When someone does attempt to capture review feedback as tickets, it happens after the meeting when details are fuzzy. The ticket says “refactor auth module” but the meeting discussed four specific methods in two specific files. That nuance is gone.

~>

No link between feedback and codebase

Even diligent note-takers can’t include the actual file paths and function signatures referenced in the discussion. Engineers receiving the ticket have to go spelunking through the repo to reconstruct what was obvious in the review session.

Code review tasks that are actually actionable

contextprompt doesn’t just transcribe — it cross-references what was said against your repo to produce tasks an engineer can start immediately.

100%

of verbal feedback captured

0

manual ticket creation

<5m

review-to-task pipeline

Every comment becomes a task

contextprompt identifies each actionable suggestion in the transcript — refactors, bug fixes, performance improvements, test gaps — and converts them into discrete, structured tasks. Nothing slips through because it was said off-hand at the end of the call.

Real file paths, not vague descriptions

Because contextprompt has scanned your connected repos, it can map “the user service auth handler” to src/services/user/auth.ts:handleLogin(). Tasks include the exact file paths, line ranges, and export names referenced in the discussion.

Ready for Claude Code or Cursor

Each task includes the source quote, mapped file paths, implementation steps, and a confidence score. Paste into Claude Code and it has everything it needs to make the change. No back-and-forth, no repo archaeology.

What a review task looks like

This is the structured output contextprompt generates from a single verbal code review comment.

// contextprompt extracted task from code review — 2026-04-13

task: Extract validation logic into reusable helper
confidence: high

files:
  src/api/routes/users.ts (lines 42-67, validateUserPayload)
  src/utils/validation.ts (create new helper here)

source quote:
  "that validation block in the users route is duplicated in three places,
  we should pull it into a shared helper before merging"
  — Sarah K., 14:23

steps:
  1. Create validateUserPayload() in src/utils/validation.ts
  2. Replace inline validation in users.ts, posts.ts, comments.ts
  3. Add unit tests in src/utils/__tests__/validation.test.ts

Your code review session, automated

Here is what a code review meeting looks like with contextprompt handling the follow-up.

1
One-time setup

Connect your repos

Add the repository being reviewed from the contextprompt dashboard. The tool indexes your file tree, export signatures, and types — giving it the context it needs to map verbal references to real code locations. Takes seconds.

2
Review starts

Paste the meeting link

Drop the Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams link into contextprompt. The AI bot joins the review session as a silent participant and captures every comment with speaker attribution and timestamp.

3
Review ends

AI extracts every action item

contextprompt processes the full transcript against your repo map. It identifies every refactor suggestion, bug fix, test gap, and architectural feedback — each mapped to the specific files and functions discussed.

4
Minutes later

Structured tasks, ready to ship

Tasks appear in the contextprompt dashboard with full context, file paths, and implementation steps. Assign directly, copy into your tracker, or paste into Claude Code to start implementing immediately.

★★★★★
“We used to lose half our code review feedback between the meeting and the ticket. Now contextprompt catches everything and I paste the tasks straight into Claude Code. The file paths it generates are spot on.”
Priya M. — Senior Engineer, fintech startup
